headline: Glaucoma Screening in KK Nagar, Chennai
subheadline: Glaucoma often has no symptoms until vision is lost. Dr. Rajeswari offers thorough screening to protect your sight.
metaDescription: Glaucoma screening and management in KK Nagar, Chennai with Dr. Rajeswari. Early detection protects vision permanently. Call or book online.

whyPatientsChoose.title: Focused on Preserving Your Vision
whyPatientsChoose.description: Our priority is catching changes early, when treatment can still protect what you have.

whyPatientsChoose.title: Convenient Ongoing Monitoring
whyPatientsChoose.description: Glaucoma care means regular checks over time -- being close to home makes that easy to keep up with.

processSteps.title: Eye Pressure Check
processSteps.description: A quick, painless measurement of intraocular pressure.

processSteps.title: Optic Nerve Evaluation
processSteps.description: Assessment of the optic nerve for early signs of damage.

processSteps.title: Ongoing Protection
processSteps.description: If needed, treatment focused on preserving the vision you have.

landingFaqs.question: If my vision is normal, why should I be screened?
landingFaqs.answer: Glaucoma typically causes no symptoms until significant, often permanent vision loss has already occurred. A simple pressure check and optic nerve evaluation can catch it years before you'd ever notice anything yourself.

landingFaqs.question: How often should glaucoma screening be done?
landingFaqs.answer: Generally every 1-2 years after age 40, or annually if you have risk factors like diabetes, high blood pressure, or a family history of glaucoma. We'll recommend a schedule based on your specific risk.

landingFaqs.question: What happens if glaucoma is detected early?
landingFaqs.answer: Most cases are managed effectively with eye drops alone. The goal of early detection is simple -- protect the vision you currently have before any is lost.

educationalInsight.title: Why Glaucoma Often Goes Unnoticed
educationalInsight.body: Many people assume that if their vision feels normal, their eyes must be healthy. Glaucoma is often called the 'silent thief of sight' precisely because it doesn't work that way -- peripheral vision is lost so gradually that most people don't notice until significant, often permanent damage has occurred. A simple eye pressure check and optic nerve evaluation can catch it years before you'd ever notice symptoms yourself. The goal of screening is not to find disease -- it's to find it early enough that the vision you have can be protected.
prioritizeIf: You are over 40 and have never had a glaucoma screening
You have a family history of glaucoma
You have diabetes or high blood pressure
It has been more than a year since your last optic nerve check

doctorsPerspectiveStructured.patientConcern: Doctor, my vision seems normal. Why should I worry about glaucoma?
doctorsPerspectiveStructured.response: This is the question I hear most often, and it's an important one. Glaucoma rarely announces itself -- by the time someone notices a change in vision, some damage has typically already happened, and that loss cannot be reversed. Screening isn't about finding something wrong. It's about making sure nothing is happening silently that we could otherwise have caught and managed in time.
